items.q: Does globalisation only mean economic trade?
items.correct: No - it also involves cultural, political and digital interconnection.
items.misconception: Globalisation is purely about money and trade.
items.why: Globalisation links flows of culture, people and information, not just goods and capital.

items.correct: The inequality between those with and without effective digital access.
items.misconception: It is just people who dislike technology.
items.random_wrong: It means everyone now has equal internet access.
items.why: The divide is about unequal access and skills, which can deepen existing social inequalities.

items.q: Does cultural globalisation make all cultures identical?
items.correct: Not entirely - shared culture spreads, but local cultures also adapt and resist (glocalisation).
items.misconception: Every culture becomes exactly the same.
items.random_wrong: Globalisation has no cultural effect at all.
items.why: Homogenisation is one trend, but hybridity and local resistance mean cultures do not simply merge.
Correct answer: Interconnection of societies worldwide. “Isolation of nations” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Harvey's 'time-space compression' describes how technology makes distant places feel:
items.why: It's Closer and more connected. “Farther apart” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: The 'digital divide' is the gap between people who:
items.correct: Do and do not have effective internet access
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Do and do not have effective internet access. “Like and dislike phones” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.correct: Spreads a similar global consumer culture
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Spreads a similar global consumer culture. “Increases local diversity” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
